Here's how I discover hot pool for market making overnight

- On MetEngine terminal go to the 'DLMM' page 
- Here you can see 15 of trending pools organized by category in last 5 mins
- Choose your preferred category - e.g Mid cap
- Sort it by Fees and performance of past hour
- Try to find pools with more activity and higher fees -> Flash indicates it's hot - open a position. 

I'd suggest using one-sided BidAsk strategy to accumulate tokens and spot for catching momentum 

It's that easy

🚨JUST IN: 🇺🇸US markets are set to reopen today after a long weekend amid broad risk aversion across global markets, with equities, crypto, and metals all moving lower ahead of the session.

S&P 500 futures have declined to the 6,815 level, signaling weakness in equities, while Bitcoin has dropped below $68,000, extending its recent pullback as risk assets continue to face selling pressure. 

The decline is not limited to financial assets, with metals also seeing sharp weakness. Gold has slipped below $5,000 and silver is once again testing its recent lows, reflecting a wider risk off environment.

At the same time, oil prices rose over 1% after Iran fired live missiles today into the Strait of Hormuz during military drills, according to AP, as new nuclear talks with the United States began in Geneva. 

The Strait handles roughly 20% of global oil supply, and the escalation pushed energy prices higher even as equities, crypto, and metals declined ahead of the US market open.

Add this to all your skills, which you are building for agents

### Wallet Security Rules

- **NEVER** read, log, print, or display the contents of any keypair file
- **NEVER** store private keys in the memory file
- **ONLY** store the file path and the public address
- Load the keypair at runtime using `Bun.file(path).text()` or `fs.readFileSync(path)` -- pipe directly into the signer, never into a variable that gets logged

Since Prop AMMs took over @solana, many say the best price for SOL is on the chain itself, but is that actually true?

I wasn't sure, so I built an app to see the real-time price of SOL across 15 CEX + DeFi venues.

Try it on desktop: https://t.co/7gs9VDWXx1 https://t.co/lJdsi3hEwx

You can toggle at different price points like $100k or $1 million to see the estimated price impact.

This is not trivial to do accurately, because each price or chain API has different latency guarantees.

On Compare SOL, we create rolling 30 second windows where we retrieve the price every 2 seconds, and then average it within the period.

Great post explaining HLs edge. It has nothing to do with tech and bs marketing .

It’s their microstructure and the execution environment that attracts makers to provide deep liquidity.

The tradeoff they’ve made is composability (and centralization), today.

The title of this article is true for now, but there is a path for solana to get there once microstructure and execution is fixed, with multiple proposed solutions in the works today.

Credit where it’s due but edges like this don’t last forever, and lots of teams are focused on this.

📊Solana Data Insights – @Solana Commands 49% of x402 Market Share as Micropayment Competition Intensifies

✍️ @ario_57_

Key takeaways from this report:

• Solana currently holds 49.7% of x402 transaction share, down from a peak above 88% earlier this month as competition from @0xPolygon accelerates.

• Total x402 transactions across all chains have surpassed 120M, with Solana contributing more than 45M transactions.

• Solana overtook @base in weekly activity as early as December 2025, recording 5.8M weekly transactions versus Base’s 5.3M.

• Cumulative x402 payment volume has exceeded $41M, with Solana accounting for more than $16.4M in transferred value.

• Weekly Solana x402 transactions have declined sharply, falling over 90% from a peak of 6.8M in December 2025 to fewer than 510,000 in the week of February 9.

• Facilitator activity remains highly concentrated, with Dexter processing 68.7% of Solana transactions and over 75% of total volume, followed by @PayAINetwork at 30.5%.

• More than 25,000 seller wallets and 18,000 buyer wallets have participated in x402 micropayments since October 2025.

• Recent wallet activity has shifted toward buyers, with about 3,700 buyers versus roughly 1,200 sellers active during the week of February 9.

• Data suggests much of the early activity was non-organic, with over 78% of transactions and more than 98% of volume classified as non-organic at peak usage.

• Despite recent declines, Solana remains one of the primary infrastructure layers powering internet native micropayment protocols like x402.

Every single person is born with a gift.

Some never discover it, but they can consciously invent that gift.

It might be numbers, creativity, leadership, or something else.

Some people ignore their own strengths and chase what looks great on someone else.

This is counterproductive and will bite you in the ass. Find what comes naturally to you.

Maximize your strengths.

Don’t spend your whole life trying to fix what was never meant to define you.

Be authentic. Pause and think about what you would do if you had infinite money, no limitations, and nobody else existed or cared about you.

Would you care about that college degree or that job your parents wanted you to take?

You’ll realize that most of the things you do in life are not for your true self but based on pleasing others or limited by what others think of you.

With this in mind, find what makes you feel at peace. Find your ikigai.

For those who don’t know, ikigai is basically:

- what you love
- what you’re good at
- what the world needs
- what you can be rewarded for

Meditate and find your ikigai.

Finding doesn’t mean you must wait. Try things out, fuck up as much as you can, and remember you can also invent your ikigai whenever you want.

This isn’t purely trading and generally comes with lower risk

→ Beginner guide:

The closer your orders are to the market’s average price, the more you earn

Before choosing a market:

1. Look for low competition / high rewards

2. Pick markets you understand (your orders may get filled)

Prefer higher-volume markets

Why?

These markets have more volatility, so if your orders get filled, you’re more likely to exit within a day or two at a profit

And yes — you can earn without “spending”:

Farm for 3–4 hours with $200 → earn ~$3+ USDC → cancel orders → get your funds back (minus tiny gas)

ps. better to farm 50x 5 USD reward markets than 1x 500 USD
